text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
As Inari One is walking in front of the station with
her friend...

text_block_list.name: Inari One
text_block_list.text:
Oh-ho? Looks like they're hostin' a rakugo
comedy event. Looks interestin'.

text_block_list.name: Umamusume A
text_block_list.text:
Look, Inari. The signboard says they'll teach us
how to use a folding fan and hand towel. What do
you think that means?

text_block_list.name: Inari One
text_block_list.text:
Well, in the world of rakugo comedy, they use
foldin' fans and hand towels to express a buncha
different things.

text_block_list.name: Inari One
text_block_list.text:
Like, they might pretend the foldin' fan is a set of
chopsticks, a brush, or even a sword. And the
hand towel can stand in as a letter, a pouch, or a
wallet.

text_block_list.name: Umamusume A
text_block_list.text:
Chopsticks? A letter? Feels like the mystery just
keeps getting deeper.

text_block_list.name: Inari One
text_block_list.text:
I guess it's pretty meaningless to try and explain
it. Somethin' like this is better seen with your own
eyes.

text_block_list.name: Inari One
text_block_list.text:
All right, let's take a quick peek. Seein' is way
better than hearin' me ramble on about it!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
While her intentions were to only take a peek,
Inari One enjoys the show so much that she loses
all track of time and ends up skipping training.
